scores.note: The film finds the load-bearing claim and keeps returning to it: the article is strongest on how late-July decisions widened a Balkan crisis inside a preloaded system, not on every deep cause equally.

It walks a real boundary case and a real failure mode: the Entente was not automatic and Britain retained freedom, while overdetermination degrades into blame-washing if the deep catalog is treated as equally earned.

In place of vague admiration, the film ties its thesis to a specific proof chain from the notes: casus belli, blank cheque, France-Russia reaffirmation, failed partial mobilization, and two-front planning.

The quantities are grounded in the notes and used to reason rather than decorate: two bad models, two causal layers, two fronts in German planning, and one or two sufficient causes in the article's own thesis.

It names the rejected alternative cleanly and costs the chosen one honestly: the systemic account explains mechanism better than a single-culprit story, but it pays with blurrier agency unless the Germany caveat stays in frame.

The close actually rules: persuasive on late-July escalation, weaker on ranking deep causes, residual risk is blame-washing, and the thing to watch is whether Germany's larger share of responsibility remains visible.

summary: This draft interrogates its subject rather than touring it. It earns the article's anti-single-cause thesis with a specific July mechanism, keeps the weak points and failure modes visible, and closes with a real adjudication instead of a flattering restatement.
